message:
Add multi-line label rendering, fix DOT parser robustness, and improve text measurement in WASM

- Support multi-line labels in layout engine by computing height based
  on line count and sizing width to the longest line
- Render multi-line text in canvas renderer for both node labels and
  edge labels, vertically centering the text block
- Fix DOT parser comment stripping to be quote-aware, preventing
  removal of text inside quoted strings that contains "//"
- Add Unicode grapheme fallback in DOT identifier normalization for
  identifiers that have no ASCII alphanumeric characters
- Fix IR builder to not set an explicit label when the label text
  matches the node ID, preventing duplicate label display
- Deduplicate sequence diagram participant labels by filtering out
  labels that match the normalized node ID
- Use real browser text measurement in WASM canvas context via
  CanvasRenderingContext2D.measureText() with fallback to the
  character-count heuristic
- Make watch mode resilient to render errors by logging and continuing
  instead of propagating the error and exiting
- Remove redundant XML text escaping in SVG tspan content since the
  Element builder already handles escaping

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
